entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: Peanut (Arachis hypogaea), the primary crop studied in the research.

entities.name: DISEASE
entities.description: General diseases that reduce peanut yield.

entities.name: DISEASE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of peanut genotypes to resist diseases.

entities.name: CULTIVATED PEANUT GENOTYPES
entities.description: Genotypes of peanut used in cultivation, some of which possess disease resistance.

entities.name: WILD ARACHIS SPECIES
entities.description: Wild species of the Arachis genus that harbor greater levels of disease resistance.

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED SELECTION
entities.description: A technique used to facilitate the combination of cultivated and wild resistance loci with adapted alleles.

entities.name: ARACHIS GENOME
entities.description: The complete set of genetic material in the Arachis genus.

entities.name: CANDIDATE GENOME REGIONS
entities.description: Specific regions of the genome identified as controlling disease resistance.

entities.name: LATE LEAF SPOT DISEASE
entities.description: A specific disease affecting peanuts for which resistance genes and QTLs were mapped.

entities.name: A-GENOME GENETIC MAP
entities.description: The genetic map of the A-genome of Arachis used for placing candidate genes and QTLs.

entities.name: MICROSATELLITE MARKERS
entities.description: Markers used to construct the A-genome genetic map, transferable within the genus Arachis.

entities.name: LEGUME ANCHOR MARKERS
entities.description: Markers used to construct the A-genome genetic map, transferable to other legumes.

entities.name: QTL
entities.description: Quantitative Trait Loci associated with disease resistance, specifically late leaf spot.

entities.name: CANDIDATE DISEASE RESISTANCE GENES
entities.description: Genes identified as potentially controlling disease resistance.

entities.name: LINKAGE GROUPS
entities.description: Groups of genes or markers on the same chromosome where candidate genes and QTLs are distributed.
